titles.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Upon Whom Hot Water is Poured on His Head, Causing His Hair to Fall Out
Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Upon Whom Hot Water is Poured on His Head, Causing His Hair to Fall Out

segments: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ صَبَّ مَاءً حَارّاً عَلَى رَأْسِ رَجُلٍ فَامْتَعَطَ شَعْرُهُ فَلاَ يَنْبُتُ أَبَداً قَالَ "عَلَيْهِ اَلدِّيَةُ".
arabic.text: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ صَبَّ مَاءً حَارّاً عَلَى رَأْسِ رَجُلٍ فَامْتَعَطَ شَعْرُهُ فَلاَ يَنْبُتُ أَبَداً قَالَ "عَلَيْهِ اَلدِّيَةُ".

segments:
Hadith.5330 - It is narrated from Ja'far ibn Bashir, from Hisham ibn Salim, from Sulayman ibn Khalid, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who poured hot water on another man's head, causing his hair to fall out and never grow back.
Imam (as) said: "He must pay the full blood money (diyyah)."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.5330 - It is narrated from Ja'far ibn Bashir, from Hisham ibn Salim, from Sulayman ibn Khalid, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who poured hot water on another man's head, causing his hair to fall out and never grow back.
Imam (as) said: "He must pay the full blood money (diyyah)."

narratorChain.raw: رَوَىٰ جَعْفَرُ بْنُ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ قَالَ:

titles.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Upon Whom Hot Water is Poured on His Head, Causing His Hair to Fall Out

segments: : أَهْرَاقَ رَجُلٌ عَلَى رَأْسِ رَجُلٍ قِدْراً فِيهَا مَرَقٌ فَذَهَبَ شَعْرُهُ فَاخْتَصَمُوا فِي ذَلِكَ إِلَى عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَأَجَّلَهُ سَنَةً فَلَمْ يَنْبُتْ شَعْرُهُ فَقَضَى عَلَيْهِ بِالدِّيَةِ.
arabic.text: : أَهْرَاقَ رَجُلٌ عَلَى رَأْسِ رَجُلٍ قِدْراً فِيهَا مَرَقٌ فَذَهَبَ شَعْرُهُ فَاخْتَصَمُوا فِي ذَلِكَ إِلَى عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَأَجَّلَهُ سَنَةً فَلَمْ يَنْبُتْ شَعْرُهُ فَقَضَى عَلَيْهِ بِالدِّيَةِ.

segments: Hadith.5331 - It is narrated from Salamah ibn Tammam that a man poured a pot of broth over another man's head, causing his hair to fall out. They disputed this matter before Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), who delayed the judgment for a year. When the man's hair did not grow back, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled that the perpetrator must pay the full blood money (diyyah).
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text: Hadith.5331 - It is narrated from Salamah ibn Tammam that a man poured a pot of broth over another man's head, causing his hair to fall out. They disputed this matter before Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), who delayed the judgment for a year. When the man's hair did not grow back, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled that the perpetrator must pay the full blood money (diyyah).

narratorChain.raw: وَرُوِيَ عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ تَمَّامٍ قَالَ:
